title: cremanthodium smithianum
intro: Cremanthodium smithianum, also known as the Tibetan daisy or creeping cremanthodium, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the Himalayan region and parts of China. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce pain, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Cremanthodium smithianum is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be consumed raw or used topically in creams and salves.
